text.title: solution-assessment Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for solution-assessment as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Capturing the baseline at assessment time rather than at requirements freeze.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The "baseline" silently becomes "yesterday" and variance is zero by construction — the assessment proves nothing and is unfalsifiable.

failure-mode.description: Treating requirements compliance as equivalent to value realization: reporting "100% requirements met" without measuring business outcomes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A solution can be 100% requirements-met and 0% value-realized; the report must surface the functional-met vs benefit-realized split explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Reporting metric variance (e.g., revenue +18%) as caused by the solution without controlling for external factors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Market tailwinds, competitor outages, or compensation-plan changes can produce the same variance. Without a control group or diff-in-diff, confidence must be marked low and external factors flagged.

failure-mode.description: Running a single 30-day post-launch assessment and treating it as definitive.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: At 30 days, users are in honeymoon mode; 12-month assessments consistently show lower satisfaction and surfaced friction that the 30-day report misses. One-shot assessments systematically overstate success.

failure-mode.description: Assessing adoption (usage rate) as a proxy for value delivered.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mandatory rollouts always show high adoption; only paired cycle-time or output metrics reveal whether the adoption was beneficial or destructive.

failure-mode.description: User satisfaction surveys that do not reconcile survey respondent count against active-user count.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Surveys reach still-active users; n=50 with a 4.2 score can hide 200 silent leavers. Always cross-reference survey n against active user count; reject satisfaction claims where n/active-users is below a documented threshold (e.g., 10%).
